     The Numbers Business Report

Ben-Hur (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $26,410,477Details
International Box Office $65,259,171
Worldwide Box Office $91,669,648
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,645,837
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,903,696
Total $5,549,533
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The story of Judah Ben-Hur, a prince falsely accused of treason by his adopted brother Messala, an officer in the Roman army. Stripped of his title, separated from his family and the woman he loves, Judah is forced into slavery. After years at sea, Judah returns to his homeland to seek revenge, but an encounter with Jesus leads him to the Crucifixion, where he discovers forgiveness and finds redemption.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$11,203,815 (42.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.36 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:28.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$95,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,084 opening theaters/3,084 max. theaters, 3.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $36,516,679

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 19th, 2016 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: June 30th, 2016 (Wide) (South Korea)
August 19th, 2016 (Wide) (Mexico)
August 19th, 2016 (Wide) (Brazil)
August 19th, 2016 (Wide) (Venezuela)
August 19th, 2016 (Wide) (Bulgaria)
Video Release: November 29th, 2016 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of violence and disturbing images.
(Rating bulletin 2432 (Cert #50500), 7/6/2016)
Running Time: 123 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Remake
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, Falsely Accused, Miscellaneous Racing, Religious, Revenge
Social setting: Adopted Family, Bible Epic, Slavery
Subgenre: Heroic Adventure
Time period setting: Ancient Rome
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Paramount Pictures, Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Pictures, Sean Daniel Company, Lightworkers Media, Timur Bekmambetov
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

The story of Judah Ben-Hur, a prince falsely accused of treason by his adopted brother Messala, an officer in the Roman army. Stripped of his title, separated from his family and the woman he loves, Judah is forced into slavery. After years at sea, Judah returns to his homeland to seek revenge, but an encounter with Jesus leads him to the Crucifixion, where he discovers forgiveness and finds redemption.
